summary: Role 7 lifecycle/sync/public-safety consensus review inspected the revised remaining-gaps plan, sync policy, verification and completion docs, health/status artifacts, review settlement state, public-data safety report, and data git hook configuration. Verdict: AGREE on the plan sufficiency for unsafe auto-sync and unreviewed promotion protection, with implementation still incomplete.
decisions: Returned AGREE because the revised plan includes public-data/sync policy gates, global safety checks, health/goal/Observatory alignment, and review/lifecycle protections against unreviewed promotion.
Current implementation remains incomplete and data state is dirty from live review artifacts, but the question was plan sufficiency, not implementation completion.
Existing docs and status files show hooks:data:verify, review settlement, worklist actions, and public-data safety are the right evidence surfaces for implementation.
next_steps: Implement hard verifier linkage so safety:public-data:check, hooks:data:verify, review settlement/worklist action status, and no-unreviewed-accepted checks are part of verify:goal.
Add explicit lifecycle/sync/public-safety status to the remaining-gaps plan if the main consensus document wants Role 7 evidence to be even more discoverable.
Keep public safety warnings visible until machine-local path and conditional-untracked warnings are either classified, redacted, or explicitly accepted as non-blocking.
